policy: Cite primary papers and official docs directly. Do not cite AI assistants as sources.

citations.title: Task-Aware LoRA Adapter Composition via Similarity Retrieval in Vector Databases
citations.authors: Riya Adsul; Balachandra Devarangadi Sunil; Isha Nalawade; Sudharshan Govindan
supports: Retrieval-based LoRA adapter composition/routing is related work.
Atlas Reader should distinguish corpus/card routing from adapter-bank routing.

citations.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
citations.authors: Akari Asai; Zeqiu Wu; Yizhong Wang; Avirup Sil; Hannaneh Hajishirzi
supports: Training models to retrieve, critique, and reflect on retrieved evidence is prior art.
Insufficient-context and critique-style behavior are related prior art.

citations.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
citations.authors: Tim Dettmers; Artidoro Pagnoni; Ari Holtzman; Luke Zettlemoyer
supports: QLoRA trains adapters through 4-bit quantized frozen models.
NF4, double quantization, and memory-efficient fine-tuning are prior art.

citations.title: ALoFTRAG: Automatic Local Fine Tuning for Retrieval Augmented Generation
supports: LoRA fine-tuning to improve RAG citation/answer accuracy is prior art.
Synthetic data generation/filtering for RAG fine-tuning is prior art.

citations.title: AutoRAG-LoRA: Hallucination-Triggered Knowledge Retuning via Lightweight Adapters
supports: RAG + LoRA adapter retuning for hallucination/factuality control is related work.
Atlas Reader LoRA should state its contribution as contract/interface discipline, not adapter retuning generally.

citations.title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
citations.authors: Darren Edge; Ha Trinh; Newman Cheng; Joshua Bradley; Alex Chao; Apurva Mody; Steven Truitt; Jonathan Larson
supports: GraphRAG / graph-structured retrieval is prior art.
Atlas should not claim structured or graph-like RAG as new.

citations.title: Hugging Face Accelerate documentation
supports: Accelerate is a training dependency used under Hugging Face training stacks.
Hardware/device orchestration should cite official docs when discussed.

citations.title: bitsandbytes Installation Guide
supports: bitsandbytes Python >= 3.10 and PyTorch >= 2.4 minimum requirements.
bitsandbytes NVIDIA CUDA support and NF4/FP4 quantization support.
Windows x86-64 CUDA wheel support ranges.
pip install bitsandbytes guidance.

citations.title: Hugging Face Datasets documentation
supports: Dataset loading and Dataset object usage in training scripts.
The training script's use of Hugging Face Datasets should cite official docs.

citations.title: PEFT LoRA documentation
supports: LoraConfig parameters: r, target_modules, lora_alpha, lora_dropout, bias, task_type.
LoRA config examples used by the training templates.

citations.title: Safetensors documentation
supports: Safetensors is included in training requirements.
Adapter/model serialization claims should cite official docs when discussed.

citations.title: Transformers bitsandbytes quantization documentation
supports: BitsAndBytesConfig.
4-bit quantization.
NF4 for training 4-bit base models.
Nested/double quantization.

citations.title: Transformers Callbacks documentation
supports: TensorBoardCallback sends Trainer logs to TensorBoard.
Trainer callbacks support logging to TensorBoard and other ML platforms.
TENSORBOARD_LOGGING_DIR / output-dir based TensorBoard logging behavior.

citations.title: Transformers Trainer documentation
supports: Gradient accumulation.
Gradient checkpointing.
Training arguments and stability settings.

citations.title: TRL SFT Trainer documentation
supports: SFTTrainer usage.
Conversational messages dataset format.
packing=True.
assistant_only_loss=True.
PEFT adapter training through SFTTrainer.

citations.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
citations.authors: Edward J. Hu; Yelong Shen; Phillip Wallis; Zeyuan Allen-Zhu; Yuanzhi Li; Shean Wang; Lu Wang; Weizhu Chen
supports: LoRA freezes base model weights and trains low-rank adapter matrices.
Atlas Reader LoRA uses LoRA as mechanism, not invention.

citations.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
citations.authors: Patrick Lewis; Ethan Perez; Aleksandra Piktus; Fabio Petroni; Vladimir Karpukhin; Naman Goyal; Heinrich Küttler; Mike Lewis; Wen-tau Yih; Tim Rocktäschel; Sebastian Riedel; Douwe Kiela
supports: RAG itself is prior art.
Atlas Reader LoRA should not claim retrieval-augmented generation as new.
RAG keeps knowledge external to the model while generation uses retrieved context.

citations.title: GNN-RAG: Graph Neural Retrieval for Large Language Model Reasoning
supports: Knowledge-graph / graph-neural retrieval for RAG is related prior art.
Graph-based retrieval and reasoning are not novel to Atlas.

citations.title: Evaluate agent workflows
supports: Agent workflows should be evaluated.
Atlas agent protocol should be treated as an evaluation arm, not a proof claim.

citations.title: Custom instructions with AGENTS.md
supports: Codex reads AGENTS.md files before doing work.
Global and project-level guidance can be layered.
Repository-level AGENTS.md can encode project norms.

citations.title: Build plugins
supports: Codex plugins use .codex-plugin/plugin.json manifests.
Plugins can include skills directories with SKILL.md files.
Repo marketplaces can expose local plugins through .agents/plugins/marketplace.json.

citations.title: Rules
supports: Codex rules control which commands can run outside the sandbox.
Rules are experimental and may change.
codex execpolicy check can test rule files.

citations.title: PyTorch Get Started Locally
supports: PyTorch Windows install guidance.
Python 3.10+ support requirement.
Pip installation from PyTorch CUDA wheel indexes.
CUDA availability verification with torch.cuda.is_available().

citations.title: How Much is Too Much? Exploring LoRA Rank Trade-offs for Retaining Knowledge and Domain Robustness
citations.authors: Darshita Rathore; Vineet Kumar; Chetna Bansal; Anindya Moitra
supports: Alias key retained because earlier related-work draft used this spelling.
LoRA rank affects downstream performance and robustness.
Cross-domain/robustness questions are legitimate to evaluate rather than assume.

citations.title: How Much is Too Much? Exploring LoRA Rank Trade-offs for Retaining Knowledge and Domain Robustness
citations.authors: Darshita Rathore; Vineet Kumar; Chetna Bansal; Anindya Moitra
supports: LoRA rank affects downstream performance and robustness.
Cross-domain/robustness questions are legitimate to evaluate rather than assume.

citations.title: RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval
citations.authors: Parth Sarthi; Salman Abdullah; Aditi Tuli; Shubh Khanna; Anna Goldie; Christopher D. Manning
supports: Tree-organized / hierarchical retrieval is prior art.
Atlas lanes/cards should be positioned as a structured interface, not the first hierarchical retrieval system.

citations.title: Parametric Retrieval-Augmented Generation using Latent Routing of LoRA Adapters
citations.authors: Zhan Su; Fengran Mo; Jian-yun Nie
supports: LoRA adapter routing / parametric RAG is related prior art.
Atlas lane routing should be framed as content-level protocol, not new adapter routing algorithm.

citations.title: Get started with TensorBoard
supports: TensorBoard as a measurement and visualization tool for ML workflows.
Scalars/Time Series dashboards for loss, metrics, training speed, and learning rate.
Launching TensorBoard with --logdir.

citations.title: Equipping Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models with Document Structure Awareness
citations.authors: Lingnan Xu; Chong Feng; Kaiyuan Zhang; Liu Zhengyong; Wenqiang Xu; Fanqing Meng
supports: Structure-aware retrieval is prior art.
Atlas lanes/cards should be framed as a structured-RAG cousin, not a new structured-RAG invention.

citations.title: RAFT: Adapting Language Model to Domain Specific RAG
citations.authors: Tianjun Zhang; Shishir G. Patil; Naman Jain; Sheng Shen; Matei Zaharia; Ion Stoica; Joseph E. Gonzalez
supports: Retrieval-aware fine-tuning is prior art.
Distractor-aware RAG training is prior art.
Atlas Reader LoRA should not claim retrieval-aware fine-tuning as new.
